title: Taking Maximum Energy From the Mystic Dungeon
content:
In a mystic dungeon, n magicians are standing in a line. Each magician has an attribute that gives you energy. Some magicians can give you negative energy, which means taking energy from you.

You have been cursed in such a way that after absorbing energy from magician i, you will be instantly transported to magician (i + k). This process will be repeated until you reach the magician where (i + k) does not exist.

In other words, you will choose a starting point and then teleport with k jumps until you reach the end of the magicians' sequence, absorbing all the energy during the journey.

You are given an array energy and an integer k. Return the maximum possible energy you can gain.

Note that when you are reach a magician, you must take energy from them, whether it is negative or positive energy.

Example 1:

Input:  energy = [5,2,-10,-5,1], k = 3

Output: 3

Explanation: We can gain a total energy of 3 by starting from magician 1 absorbing 2 + 1 = 3.

Example 2:

Input: energy = [-2,-3,-1], k = 2

Output: -1

Explanation: We can gain a total energy of -1 by starting from magician 2.

Constraints:

• 1 <= energy.length <= 105

• -1000 <= energy[i] <= 1000

• 1 <= k <= energy.length - 1

​​​​​​

codeSnippets.code:
class Solution(object):
    def maximumEnergy(self, energy, k):
        """
        :type energy: List[int]
        :type k: int
        :rtype: int
        """

hints: Let dp[i] denote the energy we gain starting from index i.
We can notice, that  dp[i] = dp[i + k] + energy[i].

solutions.python:
class Solution(object):
    def maximumEnergy(self, energy, k):
        n = len(energy)
        dp = list(energy)  # dp[i] = total energy starting from i
        for i in range(n - k - 1, -1, -1):
            dp[i] = energy[i] + dp[i + k]
        # We can only start at indices 0..k-1 if we want to reach the end
        # Actually we can start at any index; we must start at one of the last k indices
        # if we want to collect fewest jumps, but we can start anywhere.
        # The chain from i visits i, i+k, i+2k, ... so chains are grouped by i % k.
        # We want the maximum dp[i] over all valid starting points.
        # A starting point i must have i + k possibly not existing (i.e., we could start anywhere).
        # Actually any index is a valid start.
        return max(dp)
solutions.explanation:
From any starting index i, we visit indices i, i+k, i+2k, ... until out of bounds. We define dp[i] as the total energy collected starting from i.

Recurrence: dp[i] = energy[i] + dp[i+k] (if i+k < n), else dp[i] = energy[i].

We fill dp from right to left. The answer is the maximum value in dp, since we can start at any index.

Note: all indices in the same chain (same i % k) form an independent sequence. For each chain, we sweep from right to left accumulating the sum, and naturally track the best starting point within the chain.

Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n).
